name: Baked Crab Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean and bake the potatoes until cooled slightly, then cut in half lengthwise.
2. Place potato pulp into a bowl, leaving about 1/4-inch of shell. Reserve the skins.
3. Mash the potato pulp.
4. Melt butter in a skillet and sauté onion for 15-20 minutes until lightly colored.
5. Add mushrooms and continue sautéing for another 5 minutes.
6. Add crabmeat and pepper to the onions. Pour in vermouth and bring to a boil, stirring frequently.
7. Cook mixture until liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat and stir in yogurt and sour cream.
8. Combine crabmeat, onion mixture, potato pulp, and about half of the cheese. Season with additional ground black pepper and half and half if desired.
9. Spoon the filling into potato skins and sprinkle with additional cheese.
10. Bake at 400°F until the cheese bubbles.
